methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $1.6B - $2.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Bright Machines
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

private.name: Carbon Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C)

data.narrative:
Nimble Robotics, a Series C robotics and manufacturing company, is evaluated against a universe of 9 comparable companies—1 public and 8 private. The peer set includes same-stage and adjacent-stage robotics firms: Exotec (Series D, $2.0B), Bedrock Robotics (Series B, $1.8B), Dexterity (Series B, $1.6B), and Hadrian (Series C, $1.6B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the company's current $1.0B post-money valuation and a typical 1.7x multiplier for Series C advancement, yields $1.7B. Peer-median analysis across the four comparable companies produces an identical $1.7B result, with peer valuations ranging from $1.6B to $2.0B. Both methods converge on the same point estimate, increasing confidence in the midpoint.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.4B (low) to $2.0B (high), with a median of $1.7B. This represents modest upside from the current $1.0B valuation. Key uncertainties include the timing and terms of the next financing round, which will ultimately validate stage-progression assumptions, and the comparability of peers across heterogeneous robotics subsectors. The tight clustering of method outputs supports the $1.7B midpoint, though the range reflects underlying uncertainty in peer selection and stage-specific step-up multiples.
